response.justification: Candidate has strong general software engineering breadth (web, backend, embedded touches) and 10+ years of experience, but lacks the core hard requirements for this role: native-level Japanese proficiency is not indicated, C for UNIX is not a primary skill (C/C++ appears only briefly at National Instruments a decade ago), and there is no demonstrated experience with Linux kernel modification, LAN-switch firmware, MACsec/802.1X, NETCONF/SNMP, or chip-vendor SDKs. The resume points to web/cloud/full-stack work, not embedded networking security.
strengths: 10+ years of software development experience across diverse stacks
Some prior C/C++ experience at National Instruments on a C IDE product
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing, dev-ops tooling
concerns: No evidence of native-level Japanese language proficiency
No Linux kernel source modification or embedded LAN-switch firmware experience
No demonstrated work with MACsec, 802.1X, NETCONF, SNMP, or chip-vendor SDKs

key_factors.factor: Limited and dated C/UNIX systems programming
